heading: Where guesthouse travelers usually stay
intro: Each island has a different feel — we'll match you based on what you're after, not just availability.

islands.name: Maafushi
islands.description: The most established guesthouse island — the widest choice of excursions and restaurants, good for first-timers.

islands.name: Guraidhoo
islands.description: Quieter than Maafushi with a similar bikini beach setup and easy access to good snorkeling sites.

islands.name: Dhigurah
islands.description: One of the longest natural beaches in the Maldives, known for regular whale shark sightings nearby.

islands.name: Fulidhoo
islands.description: Small, quiet, and known for cultural evenings (Bodu Beru drumming) — a slower-paced choice.

islands.name: Dharavandhoo
islands.description: Close to Hanifaru Bay's manta ray season — a strong pick if snorkeling with mantas is the priority.

islands.name: Gan (Laamu)
islands.description: Further from Male but connected by causeway to several other islands — good for a quieter, less touristy stay.
